children.heading: Center
children.label: 1 Center
children.text: The term “Center” means a Microelectronics Science Research Center established pursuant to subsection (d).

children.heading: Department
children.label: 2 Department
children.text: The term “Department” means the Department of Energy.

children.heading: Director
children.label: 3 Director
children.text: The term “Director” means the Director of the Office of Science.

children.heading: Historically Black college or university
children.label: 4 Historically Black college or university
children.text: The term “historically Black college or university” has the meaning given the term “part B institution” in .section 1061 of title 20

children.heading: Institution of higher education
children.label: 5 Institution of higher education
children.text: The term “institution of higher education” has the meaning given the term in .section 1001(a) of title 20

children.heading: National Laboratory
children.label: 8 National Laboratory
children.text: The term “National Laboratory” has the meaning given the term in .section 15801 of this title

children.heading: Program
children.label: 9 Program
children.text: The term “program” means the program established under subsection (c)(1).

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 10 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Energy.

children.heading: Skilled technical workforce
children.label: 11 Skilled technical workforce
children.text: The term “skilled technical workforce” has the meaning given the term in section 4(b)(3) of the Innovations in Mentoring, Training, and Apprenticeships Act ( note; ).42 U.S.C. 1862pPublic Law 115–402

children.heading: Tribal College or University
children.label: 12 Tribal College or University
children.text: The term “Tribal College or University” has the meaning given the term in .section 1059c of title 20

children.heading: Work-based learning
children.label: 13 Work-based learning
children.text: The term “work-based learning” has the meaning given the term in .section 2302 of title 20

the coming end of Moore’s Law presents major technological challenges and opportunities for the United States and has important implications for national security, economic competitiveness, and scientific discovery;

future progress and innovation in microelectronics, and the maintenance of a robust domestic microelectronics supply chain, will require an approach that advances relevant materials science, electronic and photonic device technologies, processing and packaging technologies, manufacturing technologies, circuit, chip, and system architecture, and software system and algorithm development in a codesign fashion;

children.label: A
children.text: overcoming foundational research challenges relevant to the topics described in paragraph (2); and

children.label: B
children.text: translating and transferring research outcomes to industry; and

the expertise and user facilities of the National Laboratories described in paragraph (3) will enable the Department to drive advances in microelectronics that are essential to meeting future needs in areas critical to the missions of the Department and the future competitiveness of the domestic microelectronics industry, including high-performance computing, emerging data-centric computing approaches and energy-efficient computing, optical sensors, sources, and wireless networks, and power electronics and electricity delivery systems.

children.label: A
children.text: accelerate underlying research and development for design, development, and manufacturability of next-generation microelectronics; and

children.label: B
children.text: ensure the global competitiveness of the United States in the field of microelectronics.

children.label: I
children.text: materials sciences, chemical sciences, and plasma science synthesis and fabrication;

children.label: II
children.text: novel microelectronics devices, including emerging memory and storage technologies;

children.label: III
children.text: diverse computing architectures and paradigms, including analog computing and edge computing;

children.label: IV
children.text: data-driven modeling and simulation;

children.label: V
children.text: integrated sensing, power harvesting, and communications;

children.label: VI
children.text: component integration and subsystems;

children.label: VII
children.text: photonic integration and packaging; and

children.label: VIII
children.text: development of codesign frameworks for all stages of microelectronics design, development, fabrication, and application;

children.label: ii
children.text: cybersecurity by design to result in trusted and resilient microelectronics;

children.label: iii
children.text: methods for leveraging advanced simulation and artificial intelligence to enhance codesign and discovery in microelectronics;

children.label: iv
children.text: in consultation with the National Institute of Standards and Technology, fabrication and processing science and metrology associated with microelectronics manufacturing, including lithography, patterning, surface deposition, etching, and cleaning;

children.label: v
children.text: approaches for optimizing system-level energy efficiency of advanced computing systems, the electrical grid, power electronics, and other energy infrastructure;

children.label: vi
children.text: approaches for enhancing the durability and lifetime of radiation-hardened electronics;

children.label: vii
children.text: enhancement of microelectronics security, including the development of integrated devices, packages, and thermal management for severe environments and national security;

children.label: viii
children.text: in coordination with other relevant initiatives of the Department, methods to improve the lifetime, maintenance, recycling, reuse, and sustainability of microelectronics components and systems, including technologies and strategies that reduce the use of energy, water, critical materials, and other commodities that the Secretary determines are vulnerable to disruption; and

children.label: ix
children.text: methods and techniques for domestic processing of materials for microelectronics and components of microelectronics.

children.label: A
children.text: support translational research and transfer of microelectronics technologies; and

children.label: B
children.text: identify emerging research and development needs of industry and government for the benefit of United States economic competitiveness.

children.label: A
children.text: workforce development through existing authorities and mechanisms available to the Department, including internships, fellowships, individual investigator grants, and other activities the Secretary determines appropriate; and

children.heading: Outreach
children.label: 5 Outreach
children.text: The Secretary shall conduct outreach to recruit applicants to the program and engage participants from all regions of the United States, especially individuals from underserved communities and groups historically underrepresented in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics.

children.label: A
children.text: coordinate across all relevant programs and offices of the Department; and

children.label: B
children.text: coordinate the research carried out under the program relating to microelectronics with activities carried out by other Federal agencies and programs relating to microelectronics research, development, manufacturing, and supply chain security, including the programs authorized under subsections (c) through (f) of .section 4656 of title 15

children.heading: Report
children.label: 7 Report
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Secretary shall submit to the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources of the Senate and the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology of the House of Representatives a report describing the goals, priorities, and anticipated outcomes of the program.August 9, 2022

children.label: A
children.text: to conduct mission-driven research to address foundational challenges in the design, development, characterization, prototyping, demonstration, and fabrication of microelectronics; and

children.label: B

children.label: A
children.text: a National Laboratory;

children.label: A
children.text: accelerating the development of new microelectronics science and technology, including materials, devices, circuits, systems, architectures, fabrication tools, processes, diagnostics, modeling, synthesis, and, in consultation with the National Institute of Standards and Technology, metrology;

children.label: B
children.text: advancing the sustainability and energy efficiency of new microelectronics devices, packages, and systems;

children.label: C
children.text: application-driven codesign and prototyping of novel devices to facilitate laboratory-to-fabrication transition;

children.label: D
children.text: advancing knowledge and experimental capabilities in surface and materials science, plasma science, and computational and theoretical methods, including artificial intelligence, multiscale codesign, and advanced supercomputing capabilities to invent and manufacture revolutionary microelectronic devices;

children.label: E
children.text: creating technology testbeds for prototyping platforms for validation and verification of new capabilities and sharing of ideas, intellectual property, and the unique facilities of the Department;

children.label: F
children.text: supporting development of cybersecurity capabilities for computing architectures that measurably improve safety and security and are adaptable for existing and future applications; and

children.label: G
children.text: supporting long-term and short-term workforce development in microelectronics.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Director shall, at such time, in such manner, and containing such information as the Director determines to be appropriate, issue a request for proposals from eligible entities described in paragraph (2) seeking to be designated as a Center.

children.heading: Competitive merit review
children.label: B Competitive merit review
children.text: The Director shall select eligible entities under subparagraph (A) through a competitive, merit-based process.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Each Center shall operate for a period of not more than 5 years, unless renewed for an additional 5-year period in accordance with clause (ii).

children.heading: Initial renewal
children.label: I Initial renewal
children.text: In the case of a Center that has operated for not more than 5 years, the Director may renew support for the Center on a merit-reviewed basis for a period of not more than 5 years.

children.heading: Termination
children.label: B Termination
children.text: Consistent with the existing authorities of the Department, the Director may terminate an underperforming Center during the performance period.

children.heading: Technology transfer
children.label: 6 Technology transfer
children.text: The Director, in coordination with the Director of the Office of Technology Transitions, shall seek to enter into partnerships with industry groups to facilitate the translation and transfer of research results produced by the Centers.

children.label: A
children.text: establish a coordinating network to coordinate cross-cutting research and foster communication and collaboration among the Centers; and

children.label: A
children.text: incorporation of undergraduate students, postdoctoral fellows, graduate students, and early career researchers, as well as elementary school, secondary school, and high school students, through opportunities such as dual-enrollment programs and work-based learning programs, as applicable;

children.label: B

children.label: C
children.text: technical training and certificate programs for the skilled technical workforce;

children.label: D

children.label: E
children.text: public outreach activities, including to students at elementary school, secondary school, high school, undergraduate, and graduate levels, which may include educational programming with an emphasis on experiential and project-based learning.

children.heading: Outreach
children.label: 9 Outreach
children.text: The Director shall support the workforce development of Centers under paragraph (8) by conducting outreach to recruit applicants and engage participants from all regions of the United States, especially individuals from underserved communities and groups historically underrepresented in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics.

children.heading: Intellectual property
children.label: 10 Intellectual property
children.text: The Secretary shall ensure that the intellectual property and value proposition created by the Centers are retained within the United States.

children.label: i

children.label: ii

children.heading: Funding
children.label: 12 Funding
children.text: Subject to the availability of appropriations, the Secretary shall use not more than $25,000,000 to fund each Center for each of fiscal years 2023 through 2027.
